revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:福山平野]]


'''Fukuyama Heiya''' (福山平野) is a {{ill|Plain|en|Plain|qid=Q160091|lt=Plain|1=Plain}} formed in the eastern part of {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, spanning the areas of {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|qid=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama, Hiroshima|1=Fukuyama, Hiroshima}} and {{ill|Fuchū, Hiroshima|en|Fuchū, Hiroshima|qid=Q686775|lt=Fuchū (Hiroshima Prefecture)|1=Fuchū, Hiroshima}}.<ref name="kotobank">{{Cite web|和書|publisher=コトバンク|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/福山平野-124163|title=福山平野|accessdate=2015-12-16}}</ref><ref name="tokuyama-u">{{Cite web|和書|publisher=徳山大学総合研究所|url=http://chaos.tokuyama-u.ac.jp/souken/gehp/ge-kohyou/h070.htm|title=広島70  福山平野|accessdate=2015-12-16}}</ref>

In a broader sense, this refers to the plain along the middle and lower reaches of the {{ill|Ashida River|en|Ashida River|qid=Q2577132|lt=Ashida River|1=Ashida River}}; in a narrower sense, the middle reaches are called the '''Kannabe Heiya''' (神辺平野), while the lower reaches are called the Fukuyama Heiya.<ref name="kotobank" /> This article covers the broader definition of the plain.

== Overview ==
Located roughly in central Chūgoku, in the {{ill|Bingo Province|en|Bingo Province|qid=Q1049113|lt=Bingo Province|1=Bingo Province}} area, this plain was created by sediment deposits from the {{ill|Ashida River|en|Ashida River|qid=Q2577132|lt=Ashida River|1=Ashida River}}, an {{ill|Ichikyu kasen|ja|一級河川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikyu kasen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一級水系|1=Classification of rivers in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2908753}}, and by reclamation of tidelands along the Seto Inland Sea.

In the broader sense, it extends from the left bank at Mezaki-chō in Fuchū, about 30 km downstream from the point where the river gradient steeply changes near the estuary,{{Sfn|国交省概要|p=85}} while in the narrower sense, it extends downstream from the constricted area at the border between the middle and lower reaches (near Kita Honjō in Fukuyama), which is the upper limit of the {{ill|River delta|en|River delta|qid=Q43197|lt=Delta|1=River delta}}.<ref name="tokuyama-u"/> Geomorphologically, the Kannabe Heiya in the middle reaches is considered a {{ill|Basin|ja|盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Basin (geomorphology)|qid=Q813672}},<ref name="tokuyama-u"/> and most of the delta section is at an elevation lower than flood-water level on the Ashida River.{{Sfn|国交省概要|p=4}} This is an {{ill|Alluvial plain|en|Alluvial plain|qid=Q656586|lt=Alluvial plain|1=Alluvial plain}}, mostly consisting of {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} and its weathered residual soil, known as {{ill|Decomposed granite|en|Decomposed granite|qid=Q3275386|lt=Masa (soil)|1=Decomposed granite}}.{{Sfn|国交省概要|p=5}}

Three to four thousand years ago, much of this region was a vast wetland or a shallow sea, historically referred to as “Ana-no-umi.”{{Sfn|国交省概要|p=4}} The ruins of {{ill|Kusado Sengen|en|Kusado Sengen|qid=Q6447737|lt=Kusado Sengen Town|1=Kusado Sengen}} occupy part of the deltaic area of the Ashida River at the edge of that former wetland.<ref name="tokuyama-u" /> Excavations at the Goryō site in the former Kannabe-chō have uncovered pottery dating back to the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}},<ref>{{Cite web|和書|publisher=朝日新聞|date=2014-12-18|url=http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASGDK5FP4GDKPITB01L.html|title=弥生土器に「屋形船」の絵　国内最古か　広島・御領遺跡|accessdate=2015-12-16}}</ref> indicating that the Kannabe Heiya had formed by ancient times. The delta appears to have taken shape in the medieval era.<ref name="tokuyama-u" />

Significant development of the Fukuyama Heiya began in the early modern period under the {{ill|Fukuyama Domain|en|Fukuyama Domain|qid=Q909278|lt=Bingo-Fukuyama Domain|1=Fukuyama Domain}}, which carried out extensive land reclamation. In modern times, further expansion has continued through landfills.<ref name="tokuyama-u" />{{Sfn|国交省概要|p=4}}

Today, it not only serves as the central area of the {{ill|Bingo Urban Area|ja|備後都市圏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bingo Urban Area|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山都市圏|1=Bingo metropolitan area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11387376}} but is also a major {{ill|Breadbasket|en|Breadbasket|qid=Q4959201|lt=Grain belt|1=Breadbasket}} within Hiroshima Prefecture.{{Sfn|国交省概要|p=33}}

|author=国土交通省
|title=芦田川水系流域及び河川の概要
|url=https://www.mlit.go.jp/river/basic_info/jigyo_keikaku/gaiyou/seibi/pdf/ashida-4.pdf
|format=PDF
|accessdate=2015-12-16
|ref={{sfnRef|国交省概要}}
}}
